name: Charred Tomato Salsa
method: Set a dry cast-iron pan or comal over high heat until very hot. Add the tomatoes cut-side down, the onion, the garlic, and the optional serrano. Leave them alone. Let them char — dark spots are the point. When the tomatoes have softened and the onion and garlic are charred in places, pull everything off. Squeeze the garlic from its skin. Rough-chop everything together or pulse briefly in a blender. Season with salt and a squeeze of lime. Leave it thick.

storage.note: Refrigerate. Gets better after an hour.

uses.label: Tacos
uses.context: Works with beef, fish, or beans. Let it be the sauce.

uses.label: Beans
uses.context: Add a few spoonfuls while the beans are still warm. They'll absorb it.

uses.label: Fish
uses.context: Particularly good with salmon or whatever's freshest at the counter.

uses.label: Eggs
uses.context: Add a spoonful to the pan when the eggs are half-set.
